name: Sugared Marshmallow Bunnies and Chicks
recipeInstructions:
Recipe by: Jo Merrill
You can buy invert sugar at cake supply stores or sometimes at large supermarkets.
1. In a mixing bowl, soak gelatin in 1/4 cup water.
2. In a saucepan, combine 1/4 cup water, sugar, and invert sugar. Heat but do not boil; pour hot syrup into the gelatin, beating slowly.
3. Gradually add corn syrup and vanilla, beating on medium-high speed of an electric mixer until the mixture is fluffy, white, and doubled in bulk.
4. Color and flavor marshmallow as desired.
5. Keep the mixing bowl in a larger bowl of very hot water to keep the marshmallow from hardening.
6. Butter Easter molds and spoon candy into the molds. Set aside for about 1 hour.
7. Remove from molds and roll in colored sugar.
8. Dry candy for a few hours before packing in tightly covered containers.
Makes 12-15 molds.
